mapper.sql: select d.buyer_dept_id as dept_id, d.buyer_id as subject_id, d.parent_id, p.dept_name as parent_name, d.ancestors, d.dept_name, d.order_num, d.leader, d.phone, d.email, d.status, d.del_flag, d.create_by, d.create_time, d.update_by, d.update_time from buyer_dept d left join buyer_dept p on p.buyer_dept_id = d.parent_id
mapper.insert: insert into buyer_dept( buyer_id, parent_id, ancestors, dept_name, order_num, leader, phone, email, status, create_by, create_time ) values ( #{subjectId}, #{parentId}, #{ancestors}, #{deptName}, #{orderNum}, #{leader}, #{phone}, #{email}, #{status}, #{createBy}, sysdate() )
mapper.update: update buyer_dept set parent_id = #{parentId}, ancestors = #{ancestors}, dept_name = #{deptName}, order_num = #{orderNum}, leader = #{leader}, phone = #{phone}, email = #{email}, status = #{status}, update_by = #{updateBy}, update_time = sysdate() where buyer_id = #{subjectId} and buyer_dept_id = #{deptId} and del_flag = '0'

update buyer_dept set ancestors = replace(ancestors, #{oldAncestors}, #{newAncestors}), update_by = #{updateBy}, update_time = sysdate() where buyer_id = #{buyerId} and del_flag = '0' and ancestors like concat(#{oldAncestors}, '%')
